title: A method of manufacturing plastic film vests with adjustable length and a half-finished vest product manufactured with adjustable length
abstract: The subject matter of the invention is a method of manufacturing plastic film vests with adjustable length and universal application and a half-finished vest product manufactured with this method. The essential idea behind the invention consists in that colourful polyethylene film half- products (1), preferably of LDPE or HDPE type with thickness from 1 Âµm to 1000 Âµm and width from 20 cm to 100 cm are subject to cutting to working length "L", whereupon both ends of such short tubings (1) are subject to welding, and then, appropriate profiled cut-outs (4) are made in upper welded end (2) as well as side cut-outs (5) profiling shoulder straps (6) of the vest, whereupon above and along the lower welded end (3) of the half-finished vest product obtained this way, a dashed line (7) together with an image of open scissors (8) is marked with the use of the silk screen printing method, and finally, the user of the purchased half- finished vest product (9) uses scissors to cut off the lower welded end along the imprinted dashed line (7) to a desired length "LI" depending on his/her height, obtaining thus the final profile of the vest (10).
claims:
Claims Patent claims 1. A method of manufacturing plastic film vests with adjustable length and arbitrary commonly known overall shapes and colours characterised in that colourful half-finished products in the form of polyethylene film tubings (1), preferably of LDPE or HDPE-type with thickness from 1 Î¼Î·Î¹ to 1000 Î¼ÏÎ¹ and width from 20 cm to 100 cm, are subject to cutting to working length "L", whereupon both ends of this short tubing (1) are subject to welding, and then appropriate profiled cut-outs (4) are made in central portion of the welded end (2) as well as side cut-outs (5) profiling shoulder straps (6) of the vest, whereupon above and along the lower welded end (3) of the half-finished vest product obtained this way, a dashed line (7) together with an image of open scissors (8) is marked with the use of the silk screen printing method, and then the user of the purchased half-finished product (9) uses scissors to cut off the lower welded end along the imprinted dashed line (7) to a desired length "LI" depending on his/her height, thus obtaining the final profile of the vest (10). 2. The method according to claim 1 characterised in that to manufacture the vest, commonly known half-products (11) are used in the form of colourful polyethylene film bags of LDPE or HDPE type with thickness from 1 Î¼Î¹Î· to 1000 Î¼Î¹Î·, width from 20 cm to 100 cm, and with the required length "L", whereas the upper end (12) of the bag is subject to welding, and then, in central portion of the weld, appropriate profiled cut-outs (22) and side cut-outs (23) are made to obtain the desired profile of shoulder straps (24) of the vest, whereupon, along the lower 

 welded end of the half-finished vest product obtained this way, dashed line (27) together with an image of open scissors (28) is marked with the use of the silk screen printing method, and then, the user of the purchased half-finished vest product (29) uses scissors to cut off the lower welded end along the imprinted dashed line (27) to the desired length "LI" depending on his/her height, obtaining thus the final profile of the vest. 3. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2 characterised in that to manufacture the vest, half-finished products are used in the form of tubings (1) or bags (11) made of polypropylene or polyester film. 4. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2 or claim 3 characterised in that in the course of making profiled cut-outs (4 or 13 or 22) in central portion of upper welds (2 or 12) and side cut-outs (5 or 14 or 23) profiling shoulder straps (6, 15, 24) of the vest, perforation holes (25) are additionally provided on the whole surface of half-finished vest products (10, 21 , and 30). 5. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2 or claim 3 or claim 4 characterised in that at least one of the front and the back outer surface of the half-finished vest product (9, 19, or 29) is provided, before cutting off the lower welded end (3, 16, or 26) of the product, with reflective strips (31) of HDPE or LDPE type polyethylene with an addition of a dye by means of sealing them in by welding or is provided with advertising inscriptions (32) of desired content and/or images of advertising figures. 6. The method according to claim 5 characterised in that reflective strips (31) and/or advertising inscriptions (32) contain a fluorescent dye or a reflective dye or a glitter. 7. A half-finished plastic film vest product with any commonly known overall shape and colour characterised in that it is made of a half- finished polyethylene product (1 or 11), preferably of LDPE and/or HDPE type with thickness from 1 Î¼Î¹Î· to 1000 Î¼Î¹Î· and width from 20 cm to 100 cm, which has profiled cut-outs (4 or 22) as well as side cutouts (5 or 23) to give the shoulder straps (6 or 24) of the vest the required profile, whereas above and along the lower end (3 or 26) of the ready-made half-finished product (9 or 29) there is at least one dashed cutting line (7 or 27). 8. The half-finished product according to claim 7 characterised in that in its central portion it has profiled cut-outs (13) and side cut-outs (14) giving the desired profile to shoulder straps (15) of the vest, whereas above and along the lower end (16) of the ready-made half- finished product ( 19) it has linear perforation (17). 9. The half-finished product according to claim 7 or claim 8 characterised in that is provided with perforation (25) on the whole of its surface. 10. The half- finished product according to claim 7 or claim 8 or claim 9 characterised in that it is made of polypropylene or polyester film. 1 1. The half- finished product according to any of claims from 7 to 10 characterised in that on at least one of its front and back outer surfaces, it has reflective strips (31) and/or inscriptions and/or images of advertising figures (32). 12. The half-finished product according to claim 1 1 characterised in that reflective strips (31) and/or advertising inscriptions (32) contain a fluorescent dye or a reflective dye or a glitter.
